specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a React TypeScript task board, the kind of application that would appear as a book companion for a full-stack React and TypeScript guide. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include typed task cards with three status columns labeled To Do, In Progress, and Done, a drag-ready column layout, a TypeScript interface badge or code panel, a new task form with field validation, filter controls by priority or assignee, and a sidebar with mock project stats and a sprint progress bar.

followUps.label: add-priority-badge
followUps.prompt: Add a coloured priority badge to each task card — Critical, High, Medium, Low — without changing the column layout.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of an Express Node.js REST API explorer, the kind of demo shipped with a Node.js web development book. Do not ask follow-up questions. It must visibly include an endpoint catalog with mock GET, POST, PUT, DELETE routes, a request builder panel with headers and body fields, a response viewer with status code badge, a middleware pipeline diagram showing auth and rate-limit layers, an error-shape reference, and environment variable setup notes for PORT and DATABASE_URL.

followUps.label: add-auth-header-flow
followUps.prompt: Now add a Bearer token auth header to the request builder and show a 401 response example in the response viewer.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a GraphQL schema explorer and query builder, the kind companion app for a hands-on GraphQL and React book. Do not ask follow-up questions. It must visibly include a schema type browser on the left, a query editor with syntax highlighting mock, a mock resolver execution result pane, a subscription status indicator, mutation examples with variable panels, and a type relationship diagram.

followUps.label: add-variables-panel
followUps.prompt: Keep the same app but make the variables panel collapsible and add JSON syntax validation feedback.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a React Hooks pattern gallery, the kind of interactive companion shipped with a React Hooks programming book. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a useEffect lifecycle visualizer with mount, update, and cleanup phases, a custom hook composition demo, a useReducer state machine card, a useContext theme switcher, a useMemo and useCallback performance comparison panel, and a dependency graph viewer.

Build the first runnable preview of a Next.js full-stack storefront book companion demonstrating real-world patterns. Do not ask follow-up questions. It must visibly include a product grid with seeded mock data, app-router navigation breadcrumbs, server component and client component status badges, an ISR vs SSR strategy selector with description cards, a cart state panel, an image optimisation note referencing next/image, and a deployment status area with Vercel and self-host options.

followUps.label: add-wishlist
followUps.prompt: Add a wishlist toggle to each product card that persists in localStorage, without changing the storefront layout.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a real-time 3D graphics playground using WebGL 2, the kind of interactive demo that accompanies a book on WebGL. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a WebGL canvas rendering a rotating 3D object, shader control sliders for ambient light intensity and diffuse colour, a wireframe toggle, a camera orbit control description, a geometry selector with cube, sphere, and torus options, and a vertex and fragment shader code display panel with syntax highlighting.

followUps.label: add-texture-upload
followUps.prompt: Add a texture upload panel that lets the user drag in an image and apply it to the 3D object as a UV-mapped texture.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a Vue.js 3 design patterns gallery, the kind of interactive companion shipped with a Vue design patterns book. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include pattern cards for Composable, Headless Component, Provider Pattern, Renderless Component, and Plugin, a live interactive demo for each pattern, a code snippet panel per pattern, a comparison note for Vue 2 Options API versus Vue 3 Composition API, and a search filter.

followUps.label: add-teleport-pattern
followUps.prompt: Add a Teleport pattern card demonstrating Vue's Teleport component with a modal that renders outside the component tree.

specs.prompt: Build the first runnable preview of a full-stack Go and React dashboard, the kind of companion app for a Go web development book. Do not ask follow-up questions. The preview must visibly include a React frontend with a data table fed by mock Go API endpoints, an API health check panel showing endpoint status, a GORM database status card, a Gin router endpoint explorer listing routes with methods and paths, a JWT auth flow diagram, and environment setup notes for PORT, DATABASE_URL, and JWT_SECRET.

followUps.label: add-middleware-chain
followUps.prompt: Add a middleware chain visualiser showing how Gin middleware layers wrap each request before reaching the handler.
